research.interface: Resource Graph REST API, Resources operation, api-version 2024-04-01

official_sources.title: Resources - Resources - REST API (Azure Resource Graph)
official_sources.used_for: POST https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.ResourceGraph/resources?api-version=2024-04-01 (current GA version), QueryRequest{query, subscriptions, managementGroups, options, facets}, QueryRequestOptions{$top 1-1000, $skip >= 0, $skipToken overriding $skip, resultFormat table|objectArray, allowPartialScopes}, QueryResponse{count, data, facets, resultTruncated, totalRecords, $skipToken}, the resultTruncated STRING enum ("true"/"false"), and the ErrorResponse{error{code, message, details[]}} shape

official_sources.title: Work with large Azure resource data sets - Azure Resource Graph
official_sources.used_for: the 1000-record default/max page size, paging by passing the returned $skipToken back with the same query and scopes, count < totalRecords as the more-data signal, and the rule that no $skipToken is emitted when resultTruncated is true or the query uses limit/sample/take or only dynamic/null output columns

official_sources.title: Guidance for throttled requests in Azure Resource Graph
official_sources.used_for: the x-ms-user-quota-remaining (int) and x-ms-user-quota-resets-after (hh:mm:ss) headers on every response, waiting out the reset window when remaining hits 0, the ARG 429 RateLimiting error body, and the guidance to stagger rather than burst queries

research.language_rationale: Go is what our inventory tooling is written in, and the Resource Graph contract here is a single JSON POST whose hard parts -- exact QueryRequest shape, opaque $skipToken echo, string-typed resultTruncated, quota headers -- are wire-level semantics. net/http + encoding/json is the idiomatic zero-dependency surface; the official armresourcegraph SDK would only wrap this one call while hiding the throttling headers the task must honor.

verified_facts: The Resources operation is POST https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.ResourceGraph/resources?api-version=2024-04-01; 2024-04-01 is the current GA api-version.

QueryRequest carries query (required), subscriptions, managementGroups, options, and facets; QueryRequestOptions supports $top (1-1000), $skip (>= 0), $skipToken (overrides $skip when present), resultFormat (table or objectArray), and allowPartialScopes.

QueryResponse carries count, totalRecords (both int64), data (an array of row objects in objectArray format), facets, resultTruncated, and $skipToken; $skipToken is present only when another page exists.
resultTruncated is a string enum with values "true" and "false", not a JSON boolean; when it is "true" no $skipToken is set.
Paging works by resending the SAME query and scopes with options.$skipToken set to the token from the previous response; each paginated call consumes one query quota.
No $skipToken is emitted when the query contains limit or sample/take operators, or when all output columns are dynamic or null typed; count < totalRecords also signals an incomplete result set.

Every response carries x-ms-user-quota-remaining (queries left in the current window, e.g. 15 per 5-second window) and x-ms-user-quota-resets-after (hh:mm:ss until the window resets); when remaining reaches 0 the client must wait out the reset before the next query.
ARG-level throttling answers 429 with an error body whose code is RateLimiting; Resource Graph errors use {error:{code, message, details:[{code, message}]}}.
Errors are returned for non-200 statuses with the ErrorResponse shape; details entries carry their own code/message pairs.
